WITN09980600
WITN09980600

269. As reported to RCC and ARC, one of the key recommendations made by Protect was in relation to POL's training and communications around whistleblowing.

270. In or about December 2020, I intervened to ensure that sufficient progress was being made in a timely manner.

271. In January 2021, a working group was set up to undertake a more comprehensive review of Speak Up investigations and Postmaster complaints. This working group looked at, amongst other things, the various ways in which Postmasters could raise complaints, which may include Speak Up concerns, and how those concerns could communicated to the Speak Up team. I was concerned that Postmasters may not be using POL's Speak Up hotline but raising concerns in other ways (given the varying ways available to them) which me or my team may not have awareness or oversight of.

272. In or about August 2021, following the Hamilton judgment, POL received a KPMG report to assess the current status of investigation processes including whistleblowing and advise on a target operating model.

273. In or about September 2021, a number of additional milestones had already been achieved:

273.1 a training workshop was run by Protect in which some members of the SEG, senior leaders and the whistleblowing team attended;

Page 106 of 124
